title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, Preconditions, and Status · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A backend pairing room predicts runtime, request, middleware, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract for http semantics, resources, methods, preconditions, and status. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.
Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method properties independently and preserve them through application effects.
Choose POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, status, Location, representation, and asynchronous completion behavior from the resource contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Resource Representation Storage
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 8048 in a partner integration using an older generated TypeScript client. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, preconditions, status, and durable effect; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Resource Representation Storage.

At Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Semantics Retry Proof, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop team handles case 6704 in a partner integration using an older generated TypeScript client. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, preconditions, status, and durable effect; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.

For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Semantics Retry Proof, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: One expected status assertion proves an endpoint obeys HTTP semantics.

stimulus.title: Semantics Retry Proof failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: One expected status assertion proves an endpoint obeys HTTP semantics.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Semantics Retry Proof happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Semantics Retry Proof condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Semantics Retry Proof
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Semantics Retry Proof: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop team handles case 8323 in a partner integration using an older generated TypeScript client. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, preconditions, status, and durable effect; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Semantics Retry Proof.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Semantics Retry Proof, keep Semantics Retry Proof connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.
